prose:
Method provenance (frontier orbital energies of pyridine)

• Property: HOMO and LUMO one-electron energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
• Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (RIJK) as implemented in PySCF
• Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz); no re-optimization was performed (notation: DFT//input xyz)
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model)
• Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
• Software/backend: PySCF (version bundled with chemkit)
• SCF convergence: converged in 10 cycles; gradient information not applicable to single-point
• Engine warnings: no warnings reported

Headline numerical results (gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP single-point)
  ‑ HOMO energy = −7.2525992 eV
  ‑ LUMO energy = −1.1127860 eV
  ‑ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV
  ‑ Koopmans vertical IP = 7.2525992 eV; EA = 1.1127860 eV

Reproducibility
The calculation was executed with chemkit skill `frontier-orbitals` using the command recorded in the JSON:
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional B3LYP --basis def2-TZVP --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp3uv5elo5/live.json
The live engine log was streamed to the standard .out file created by chemkit during execution (path available in the working directory). The structured result is saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp3uv5elo5/live.json.

No warnings were emitted; integrity check status: ok (trustworthy: true).

These orbital energies are method- and geometry-specific (B3LYP/def2-TZVP on the provided geometry) and should not be compared directly to values obtained at different levels of theory without proper alignment of references.
